page: Amazon Web Services c6id.32xlarge is a compute-optimized instance powered by the x86_64 architecture. It features an Intel Xeon 8375C processor with 128 virtual CPUs (64 physical cores) running at 3.5 GHz, supported by 256 GB of DDR4 memory. This instance includes 7600 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 50 Gbps, running on the AWS Nitro hypervisor.

Benchmark results indicate top-tier multi-core performance, with the Passmark CPU score, extended instructions, and encryption tests all placing in the top 10%. Multi-core stress-ng and Geekbench multi-core scores rank in the top 25%, alongside strong memory bandwidth metrics for both cached and uncached read/write operations. Single-core performance, including Geekbench single-core and gzip compression, remains in the middle 50% tier.

From a resource density perspective, the c6id.32xlarge offers a high ratio of compute power and substantial bundled local NVMe storage, making it highly efficient for workloads requiring high-speed local scratch space. However, with 2.0 GB of RAM per core, it is less suited for memory-bound applications compared to memory-optimized instances, representing a tradeoff where raw compute and fast local storage are prioritized over high memory-to-vCPU ratios.

This instance is optimized for compute-intensive workloads that also require high-speed, low-latency local storage. Appropriate workloads include database operations, software compilation, high-throughput web serving, Redis deployments, and medium-scale LLM prompt processing.
description: Amazon Web Services c6id.32xlarge is an x86_64 compute-optimized instance featuring 128 vCPUs (64 physical cores) powered by an Intel Xeon 8375C processor running at 3.5 GHz. It is equipped with 256 GB of DDR4 memory, 7600 GB of bundled local NVMe SSD storage, and a 50 Gbps baseline network interface. Operating on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, this instance delivers top-tier multi-core CPU performance, ranking in the top 10% on Passmark CPU and encryption benchmarks, and the top 25% for multi-core stress-ng and memory bandwidth tests. While single-core performance sits in the average tier, its high compute density and massive local storage make it highly cost-efficient for data-intensive applications. The c6id.32xlarge is best suited for high-throughput database operations, software compilation, Redis deployments, and compute-heavy workloads requiring fast local scratch space.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c6id.32xlarge is a compute-optimized instance with 128 vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and 7600 GB local NVMe SSD storage, delivering top-tier multi-core performance for database and compilation workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c6id.32xlarge features 128 vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and 7600 GB NVMe storage, optimized for high-compute and database workloads.
tagline: A high-density compute instance featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors, massive local NVMe storage, and high-speed multi-core performance.
bullet_points: Powered by 128 vCPUs (Intel Xeon 8375C) and 256 GB DDR4 RAM
Includes 7600 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD storage
Delivers top-tier multi-core CPU and encryption performance
Features 50 Gbps baseline network bandwidth on the Nitro hypervisor
Optimized for databases, software compilation, and Redis deployments
